summary: iOS | Intermittent | DFU/Language Change fails at >90%
scenario: DFU or Language Change fails at >90% completion, causing firmware to revert after restart or language to revert after brief use
description_clean: Intermittent DFU (Device Firmware Update) or Language Change fails at >90% completion. After a failed DFU, the device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After a failed Language Change, the device may briefly use the new language then restart and revert to the previous language. This issue is intermittent and does not occur every time.
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on your Android device.
2. Navigate to the Home screen and ensure your Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings by tapping on the connected [Device Name].
4. Initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) by selecting Firmware Update and following the on-screen prompts.
5. Observe the update progress bar; allow the update to proceed past 90% completion.
6. Wait for the update to complete or fail. If the update fails, note the device status.
7. After the failed DFU, go to Device Settings → Firmware Update and check the reported firmware version (should show as up to date).
8. Restart the Poly audio device by pressing and holding the Power button until it powers off, then press the Power button again to turn it back on.
9. Reconnect the device to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
10. Navigate to Device Settings → Firmware Update and check the firmware version again.
11. Alternatively, for Language Change: Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Audio Settings → Language.
12. Select a new language from the available options and confirm the change.
13. Observe the device behavior: it may briefly use the new language, then restart and revert to the previous language.
14. Repeat steps 4-13 multiple times to confirm the intermittent nature of the issue.
expected: The DFU or Language Change should complete successfully at any percentage, including above 90%. After a successful DFU, the device should retain the updated firmware even after a restart. After a successful Language Change, the device should persistently use the new language across restarts.
actual: Intermittently, the DFU or Language Change fails at >90% completion. After a failed DFU, the device shows as up to date but reverts to outdated firmware after restart. After a failed Language Change, the device may briefly use the new language then restart and revert to the previous language.
